title: thymus serpyllum f. ortmannianus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. ortmannianus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading variety of the thyme plant that is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils, particularly thymol. The medicinal properties of creeping thyme have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and boosting the immune system. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations.
